article_meta.title: Trump’s Texas Deal Dilemma It’s Not Islamophobia, It’s Islamo-I’m-Sick-of-Hearing-About-It CNN Proves False Narratives A

reasoning: The model correctly focused on the author’s own prose and flagged genuinely biased passages without attributing quoted/source text as author bias. However, it used non-standard bias type labels not in the allowed taxonomy and missed several additional clear instances of biased authorial framing.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All flagged excerpts are from the author/editorial voice; no quoted or attributed source language was flagged.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Each flagged passage contains clear partisan, loaded, or dismissive authorial language—defensible bias identifications.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed other significant biased phrases in the author’s prose, e.g., 'did battle,' 'Oh, the media ate it up,' and categorical predictions about deportations.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Used labels outside the allowed set (e.g., 'Loaded Language,' 'Partisan Framing,' 'Dismissive Attitude') instead of taxonomy terms like Word Choice, Spin, Slant, Sensationalism/Emotionalism.
